# the hybrid full-stack operator — Operator Archetype

> **Tier:** External · **Archetype slug:** hybrid-full-stack-operator
> **Primary lever:** Cash-flow + Audience (dual, simultaneous)
> **Lead-gen channel(s):** Outbound DM ops (survival) + LinkedIn organic content (compounding) + DM-from-content (Phase 2+) + Program tier acquisition (Phase 3)
> **Offer tier:** $5K-$15K/mo retainer (Phase 1-2) + $7K-$15K program seat (Phase 3)
> **Time-to-revenue (typical):** Week 4 (outbound) + Month 5-7 (content inbound starts) + Month 16-18 (program tier opens)
> **Activation signals:** see frontmatter `activation_signals`.

> **Why this matters:** the hybrid full-stack operator is the most demanding archetype in operator-hours but produces the highest-LTV exit and the most defensible market position. They run BOTH outbound-led survival AND inbound-led compounding from day 1 — the engines feed each other. Outbound revenue funds the content runway; content audience eventually feeds the program tier; case studies from retainer work fuel both content and curriculum. Most operators who attempt this archetype fail in Phase 1 because they under-estimate the dual-load. The ones who survive build $1M-$2M operations with two revenue legs and a defensible category position. This is the archetype the workspace's `hybrid-full-stack.md` playbook operationalizes.

## Source / Lineage

This archetype is the convergence of three methodology streams:

- **The outbound-DM operator stream** — intent-stratified DM sequences, qualification-thread architecture, booking-gate engineering, volume-with-personalization. Source: 4+ public outbound-led B2B agency operators, 2022-2026.
- **The inbound-content-authority stream** — voice-extraction-first content, pillar-mix discipline, comment-reply harvest, DM-from-content gating. Source: 6+ public inbound-led B2B agency operators, 2023-2026.
- **The program-mentor-architect stream** — case-study-density requirements, cohort cadence, first-win triggers, curriculum design from validated transformations. Source: 5+ operators who transitioned from agency-only to agency-plus-program, 2023-2026.

Synthesized from public operator content, podcast appearances, and operator teardowns. No client engagement data, no private partnership data, no proprietary information used. The archetype description triangulates against the workspace's own analyzed corpus + the `hybrid-full-stack.md` playbook (which is the operationalization of this archetype).

## Background

**Who this archetype is.** Founder-operator of a B2B LinkedIn marketing agency who runs both outbound DM operations AND inbound content compounding from day 1, with explicit phase-shifts in resource allocation as the agency matures. Eventually layers a high-ticket program tier on top of the agency, producing two revenue legs that cross-pollinate.

**Track record (typical at maturity, 24-36 months in).**
- Retainer book: 8-15 clients at $5K-$15K/mo ($60K-$180K MRR from services)
- Program tier: 2-4 cohorts/year at $7K-$15K × 8-15 students ($150K-$600K annual program revenue)
- Annual revenue: $700K-$2M typical at maturity (50-60% retainer + 40-50% program)
- LinkedIn audience: 20K-80K followers
- Outbound volume: 100-150/week sustained in Phase 2; tapers to 50/week of select-target outbound in Phase 3
- Inbound DMs from content: 25-60/month at maturity
- Team: operator + 1-2 VAs + 1 ghostwriter + 1 program-success operator

**How they got there.** The 3-phase arc that `hybrid-full-stack.md` operationalizes. Phase 1 (months 1-4) is outbound-led survival: 60% of time on outbound ops, 25% on content seeding, 10% on foundations, 5% on systems. Phase 2 (months 5-12) is the hybrid-balance window: 35% outbound, 35% content + engagement, 20% client fulfillment, 10% systems. Phase 3 (months 13-24+) is inbound-led with program launch: 15% outbound (select targets only), 30% content, 25% fulfillment, 20% program delivery, 10% strategy.

## Core Thesis

**"Outbound is for survival; content is for compounding; program is for monetization. Run all three on a phase-shifted resource curve, not one at a time."**

Unpacked:

1. The operators who pick one leg under-perform the operators who run the right ratio. Outbound-only operators cap at $30K-$60K MRR and burn out in year 2 (no compounding asset). Content-only operators starve in year 1 (no revenue while audience builds). Program-only operators have no proof pool (no validated case studies). The hybrid runs all three with the right resource ratio per phase.
2. The engines are not independent — they feed each other. Outbound revenue funds the content runway. Content audience eventually feeds the program tier. Case studies from retainer work fuel both content (proof posts) and curriculum (program modules). Skipping any leg starves the others within 12-18 months.
3. The hardest part is Phase 1 dual-load. Operators who survive months 1-4 of running outbound at volume WHILE shipping 5-7 content posts/week WHILE doing voice extraction + ICP work WHILE closing first 3-5 retainer clients have proven they can run the rest of the arc. Phase 1 is the filter.
4. The program tier is not optional — it's where the LTV math closes. At maturity, 40-50% of revenue comes from program. Without program, the operator caps at retainer-book ceiling (10-15 clients × $10K = $1.2M ARR before capacity-trapped). With program, the same operator can run a 10-12 client retainer book at $1M ARR + $500K-$1M program revenue without scaling fulfillment headcount proportionally. That's the high-LTV exit.

## When this archetype fits

Adopt this archetype when ALL of the following hold:

- Operator has runway of ≥6 months OR willing to fund runway from outbound-driven revenue in months 1-4
- Operator is willing to be the public face of the agency on LinkedIn (content) AND personally send DMs at volume (outbound)
- Operator can sustain 25-35 hrs/week on combined ops, with explicit VA support coming online by month 4-6
- Vertical addressable audience on LinkedIn is ≥50K (content compounding math requires this minimum)
- Operator has at minimum 1 validated case study OR a credible "doing-the-work-publicly" thesis (early proof for both content and outbound qualification)
- Operator's short-term goals include BOTH "sign clients in 30-60 days" AND "build compounding asset by Year 2" — the dual-goal is the signal

Don't adopt this archetype when:

- Operator runway <90 days AND no outbound experience — Phase 1 dual-load will collapse the operator
- Operator unwilling to be public face — kills inbound leg, route to `outbound-dm-operator`
- Operator unwilling to do cold outbound — kills survival leg, route to `inbound-content-authority` (only if runway covers 12 months)
- Vertical addressable audience <50K — route to vertical-aggregation strategy or outbound-only
- Operator solo, <40 hrs/week available, AND no plan to hire VA by month 4-6 — dual-load is unsustainable

## The arc

```
Phase 1 (Months 1-4):    Outbound-led survival + content seeding
Phase 2 (Months 5-12):   Hybrid balance + audience compounding
Phase 3 (Months 13-24+): Inbound-led + program tier launch
```

The phases are not gates — they're shifts in resource allocation as pipeline composition changes. Operators who treat them as gates ("I'll start content once outbound is humming") never actually start content. Operators who try to skip Phase 1 ("I'll go content-pure from day 1") burn out by month 9 with no revenue.

See `reference/playbooks/hybrid-full-stack.md` for the full phase-by-phase operational detail. This archetype file describes the *operator profile* that runs the playbook; the playbook describes the *operations* the operator runs.

## The decision tree

Per phase, the load-bearing decisions:

**Phase 1 decisions:**
- Do I delay content to focus on outbound? → No. Seed content now even if it doesn't convert. Audience compounds on time-in-market.
- Do I close a sub-ICP client to hit revenue? → No. The first 3-5 case studies define future positioning. Hold pricing, hold ICP fit.
- Do I hire a VA in month 2? → Only if outbound volume sustained ≥150 sends/week. Otherwise wait until month 4.

**Phase 2 decisions:**
- DM-from-content rate is stuck at <5/month in month 7. Do I push more posts? → No. Audit pillar mix first. Likely Authority + Personality drift.
- Outbound and inbound calls together exceed operator capacity. Which to cut first? → Cut outbound. Inbound calls close at 45-60% vs. outbound 25-40%. Margin per hour is 2-3× better on inbound.
- Operator burnout signal in month 9-10. What to cut? → Cut outbound to 50/week of select targets. Hire VA for engagement-harvest. Do not cut content cadence.

**Phase 3 decisions:**
- When to launch program tier? → 8-15 validated case studies with full transformation metrics + operator/client sign-off for program use. Earlier launches have thin proof and high refund rates.
- Cohort size for first launch? → 8-15. Build cohort discipline before scaling. Larger first cohorts collapse the first-win-trigger work.
- Should I cap the retainer book? → Yes. 10-15 clients max. Above this, voice-drift discipline degrades and operator splits attention from program quality.

## The content engine

This archetype's content engine has phase-shifted pillar mixes:

- **Phase 1 pillar mix:** 30% Authority, 30% Personality, 25% Proof, 10% Pier, 5% Offer-bridge. Light Offer-bridge because no audience to convert yet — front-loading offer content burns the small early audience.
- **Phase 2 pillar mix:** 35% Authority, 25% Personality, 25% Proof, 10% Offer-bridge, 5% Pier. Authority increases as case studies stack; Offer-bridge doubles as audience size makes the conversion math work.
- **Phase 3 pillar mix:** 35% Authority, 20% Personality, 20% Proof, 15% Offer-bridge, 10% Pier (now split 50/50 between agency offer + program offer). Pier mechanism shifts to category-defining content that anchors the operator's program positioning.

Cadence: 5-7 posts/week sustained from week 3 of Phase 1. Comment-reply harvest in the first 60 minutes after publish is non-negotiable from month 1. Operators who start content "casually" and ramp later never compound — the audience requires the cadence signal.

Voice-extraction is FIRST. Per `INVARIANTS.md` A-5, the dependency chain is sacred: ICP → Voice → Content → Conversion artifact. Operators who skip voice-extraction to "start posting" produce 6-9 months of generic-LinkedIn-voice content that accumulates engagement without conversion intent.

## The acquisition engine

Two layers running simultaneously:

**Outbound layer (volume work):**
- Phase 1: 200-300 cold sends/week sustained. Operator sends M1 personalization manually for first 3 weeks (calibrates the workspace + builds reps). VA takes M1 personalization at scale by month 2-3.
- Intent Taxonomy classification mandatory per `INVARIANTS.md` N-5. Cold / Warm / Liked-your-post / Comment-for-lead-magnet — different sequence per class.
- Booking gate: application form before calendar. No exceptions per `INVARIANTS.md` N-12.
- Phase 2: outbound volume drops to 100-150/week as inbound replaces some volume.
- Phase 3: outbound at 50/week of select high-value targets only. No cold volume — inbound carries the pipeline.

**Inbound layer (content + DM gate):**
- Every inbound DM gate-routed: prospect must reference a specific post or piece of content in the opener. Vibe-DMs route to application form.
- Application form gates the calendar even for high-intent inbound. The application surfaces firmographic fit, trigger event, budget, and decision-making process.
- Hard-DQs route to operator per `INVARIANTS.md` A-13 — founder-led DQ stays a founder call.
- Discovery call recording → content engine pipeline: every call (with permission) recorded; 3-5 best lines from prospect become hooks/bodies/proof-anchors for next month's content.

## The fulfillment shape

DFY service shape (Phase 1-2 dominant, Phase 3 stable):

- **Service tier:** $5K-$15K/mo retainer. Includes founder-voice ghostwriting (5-7 posts/week per client), DM ops for the client's account (intent-classified outbound + inbound triage), monthly reporting with attribution to pipeline + closes.
- **Voice-extraction onboarding:** 4-week process with 2-3 voice interviews, past-content audit, banned-vocabulary capture, signature-phrase library build. Per `INVARIANTS.md` N-1, no ghostwriting begins until client-voice profile ≥85%.
- **Voice-drift discipline:** weekly per-client audit per `INVARIANTS.md` A-12. Ghostwriter reviews last 7 ghostwritten posts vs. validated voice profile; flags drift > threshold; queues re-extraction interview if drift severe.
- **Capacity ceiling:** 10-15 clients max per operator + ghostwriter team. Above this, voice-drift discipline degrades and churn rises.
- **Margin target:** ≥65% gross margin at maturity. Below 60%, operator is capacity-trapped — re-run `/design-retainer-offer` with stricter target_avatar.

## The program shape

Phase 3 program tier — not optional, that's where the LTV math closes:

- **Format:** 12-16 week cohort. Weekly group call + 1:1 mentorship cadence + private community (Slack or Circle).
- **Cohort size:** 8-15 students for first cohort (build cohort discipline before scaling). Cohort 2-3 can scale to 20-25 if first-win rate ≥70%.
- **Pricing:** $7K-$15K per student. Pricing reflects: case-study density of the agency, operator's category authority at time of launch, vertical-specificity of curriculum.
- **Curriculum:** built from the 8-15 validated case studies accumulated through Phase 1-2. Modules map directly to phases of the agency's own playbook.
- **First-win trigger:** explicit per-student first-win definition by week 3-4. First-win rate target ≥70% per `/build-first-win-trigger`. Below 50%, halt cohort 2 and refresh case studies before relaunching.
- **Case-study compounding:** program graduates become next-cohort case-study material AND retainer-tier referral sources (network effect that strengthens both legs).

## Monetization mechanics

At maturity, the revenue split tends toward 50-60% retainer + 40-50% program. This split keeps both engines healthy:

- >70% retainer = capacity-trapped (no margin to scale program; operator burns out at retainer ceiling)
- >60% program = case-study pool degrades (no fresh proof for next cohort; refund rate rises)

Annual revenue at maturity: $700K-$2M typical. The high end of the range comes from:
- Vertical-specific positioning (not generic LinkedIn services)
- Operator-as-public-face (program tier prices on operator authority)
- Case-study density that compounds across both legs

## Risk profile

This archetype has the highest absolute risk-cost in operator-hours during Phase 1 but the lowest risk of strategic dead-end at maturity. Specific risks:

1. **Phase 1 dual-load burnout.** Most common failure. Operator runs 50+ hrs/week trying to do everything; crashes month 3-4. Guard: explicit weekly hour cap; hire VA by month 4 if outbound volume sustained.
2. **Half-stack pretender drift.** Operator claims hybrid but actually only runs outbound, treating content as afterthought. Symptom: content cadence lapses, posts ship 2x/week instead of 5-7x. Result: Phase 2 inbound never materializes. Guard: weekly content-cadence audit; if cadence drops, halt outbound and refocus.
3. **Voice-extraction skipped under Phase 1 pressure.** Operator skips `/extract-founder-voice` to "start posting" because outbound volume demands attention. Result: 6-9 months of generic content that doesn't convert in Phase 2. Guard: voice-extraction is the FIRST skill, no exceptions per `INVARIANTS.md` A-5.
4. **Program launched too early.** Operator launches at 4-5 case studies because Phase 2 program-tier margin is attractive. Result: thin proof pool, conversion drops, refund rate rises in cohort 1. Guard: 8-15 validated case studies before program launch.
5. **Perpetual Phase 1.** Operator runs outbound forever, never transitions to inbound-led, never launches program. Symptom: 18+ months in, still 60% outbound. Result: caps at retainer-book ceiling, burns out. Guard: phase-transition diagnostic at month 5 and month 13.
6. **Comment-pod temptation in Phase 2 valley.** Operator joins a pod to accelerate engagement in months 4-9 (the demoralizing valley before content compounds). Result: 6-9 months later, engagement collapses as LinkedIn shadow-bans. Guard: explicit no-pod commitment in operator onboarding.

## Adopt-or-don't-adopt criteria

Adopt this archetype if:
- Runway ≥6 months OR willing to fund runway from outbound (Phase 1 survival math)
- Willing to be public face (content leg requires operator presence)
- Can sustain 25-35 hrs/week with VA support coming online by month 4-6
- Vertical addressable audience ≥50K on LinkedIn
- ≥1 validated case study OR credible "doing-the-work-publicly" thesis
- Comfortable holding two operating modes simultaneously (outbound discipline + content discipline are different cognitive registers)

Don't adopt this archetype if:
- Runway <90 days AND no outbound chops (cannot survive Phase 1 dual-load) → route to `outbound-dm-operator`
- Unwilling to be public face → route to `outbound-dm-operator` or anonymous-brand strategy
- Unwilling to do cold outbound → route to `inbound-content-authority` (only if runway ≥12 months)
- Vertical <50K addressable on LinkedIn → route to vertical-aggregation strategy
- Solo, <40 hrs/week, no VA budget → route to `outbound-dm-operator` Year 1, transition to hybrid Year 2
- Allergic to discomfort of running both engines simultaneously → most common adoption failure; flag explicitly

## Tactical details (specific to this archetype)

Five micro-tactics this archetype uses that are easy to miss when looking at the high-level archetype:

1. **The "Phase 1 calendar block" tactic.** The operator hard-blocks the calendar in Phase 1: outbound block (8-11 AM, M-F), content writing block (Sunday 2-6 PM), comment-reply harvest (1 hour after each post publishes), discovery calls (Tu-Th afternoons only). Operators who try to fit hybrid into "whenever" never sustain the cadence. The calendar IS the operating system in Phase 1.

2. **The "outbound-as-content-input" tactic.** Outbound DM conversations surface ICP language verbatim — pain phrases, objection patterns, trigger events. The operator (or VA) extracts these from DM transcripts weekly and feeds them into the content engine's hook library. Outbound becomes a voice-of-customer pipeline that sharpens content even before Phase 2 inbound materializes.

3. **The "case-study capture-by-week-2" rule.** Every closed deal gets a case-study skeleton built within 2 weeks of close — anonymized firmographics, baseline metrics, transformation hypothesis, agreed-upon success metric. The full case study is filled in over 90 days as transformation data lands. Operators who skip this capture-discipline find themselves at month 12 with 8 closed clients and 2 case studies (the rest forgotten or un-anonymized).

4. **The "phase-transition diagnostic" cadence.** At month 5 (Phase 1 → 2) and month 13 (Phase 2 → 3), the operator runs a formal diagnostic: revenue composition, hours allocation, content cadence, case-study density, profile completeness across all 6 profiles. The diagnostic produces a one-page brief that drives the next phase's resource allocation. Without this, operators drift into perpetual-Phase-1.

5. **The "two-leg attribution" reporting discipline.** Every signed retainer client is tagged with origin (outbound, inbound-content, inbound-referral, program-graduate). Monthly reporting tracks the share. By month 12, the inbound share should be ≥40% of new closes. By month 18, ≥60%. By month 24, ≥75%. Operators who skip this attribution discipline cannot detect when the content engine is failing — they just see "revenue is fine" and don't notice that all of it is outbound.

## Cadence (typical operating week)

Per phase, the typical operator week:

**Phase 1 cadence (45-50 hrs/wk operator + 10-15 VA hrs/wk by month 3):**
| Block | Hours/week | Activity |
|---|---|---|
| Outbound DM ops | 18-22 | Lead-list build, M1 personalization, sequence sends, reply triage, qual threads |
| Content writing + publish | 5-7 | Sunday batch + mid-week refresh; 5-7 posts produced |
| Comment-reply harvest | 4-6 | First 60 min after publish + second wave; 5 days/week |
| Discovery calls + closes | 6-10 | 4-8 calls/week typical; closes at 25-40% |
| Foundations + systems | 4-6 | Voice extraction, ICP refinement, profile encoding |
| Strategy + planning | 2-3 | Weekly review, monthly cadence audit |

**Phase 2 cadence (35-40 hrs/wk operator + 20-25 VA hrs/wk):**
| Block | Hours/week | Activity |
|---|---|---|
| Outbound DM ops | 8-10 | Volume drops to 100-150/week; VA handles M1 personalization |
| Content writing + publish | 5-7 | Sunday batch + Tuesday refresh; cadence sustained |
| Comment-reply harvest | 5-7 | First 60 min after publish + second wave 4-8 hrs later |
| DM triage + content-DM gate | 3-5 | Daily morning block; classifies + routes inbound DMs |
| Discovery calls + closes | 6-10 | Mix of inbound + outbound; inbound closes at 45-60% |
| Client fulfillment + voice-drift | 4-6 | Weekly per-client audits + post-approval workflow |
| Foundations + systems | 2-3 | Profile refresh, case-study capture |

**Phase 3 cadence (30-35 hrs/wk operator + 30-40 VA + ghostwriter + program-success hrs/wk):**
| Block | Hours/week | Activity |
|---|---|---|
| Select outbound (no cold volume) | 2-4 | High-value targets only |
| Content + engagement | 8-10 | Cadence sustained; pillar mix tilts to Authority + Offer-bridge |
| Discovery calls + closes | 4-6 | 70%+ from inbound at this point |
| Client fulfillment oversight | 4-6 | Ghostwriter manages day-to-day; operator owns voice-drift sign-off |
| Program delivery | 8-12 | Weekly group call + 1:1 mentorship + community + curriculum refresh |
| Strategy + foundation refresh | 4-6 | Quarterly strategic refresh + per-cohort retro |

## Workspace integration

This archetype is the workspace's *default* archetype. Most agents and skills assume an operator running this archetype unless context flags otherwise.

- **Skills that load-bear for this archetype:** `/extract-founder-voice`, `/build-icp` (run twice: agency ICP + per-client ICP), `/design-retainer-offer`, `/build-application`, `/build-outbound-engine`, `/build-content-engine`, `/build-hook-library`, `/voice-drift-detector`, `/design-program-offer` (Phase 3), `/build-first-win-trigger` (Phase 3)
- **Agents:** ALL 5 department heads activate for this archetype across phases. `agency-director` runs the phase-transition diagnostic at month 5 and month 13. `foundations-head` carries Phase 1 weight. `acquisition-head` carries Phase 1-2 weight. `marketing-head` carries Phase 2-3 weight. `fulfillment-head` ramps from Phase 1 onward. `program-head` activates Phase 3.
- **Knowledge layer:** This archetype is the one `reference/playbooks/hybrid-full-stack.md` operationalizes. Read together — the archetype describes the operator profile; the playbook describes the operations.
- **Anti-pattern guard:** When operator has <90 days runway AND no outbound chops, the workspace flags `dual-load-mismatch` and recommends `outbound-dm-operator` for Year 1 with explicit content-seeding addendum.
- **Phase-transition triggers:** `paperclip.manifest.yaml` runtime triggers at month 5 and month 13 to enforce phase-transition diagnostic — without these, operators drift into perpetual-Phase-1.
